titel: The Swedish National Audit Office's report on general state subsidies as a stabilization policy instrument.
html:
In this document, the government addresses the observations and recommendations made by the Swedish National Audit Office (Riksrevisionen) in its report "General State Grants as a Stabilizing Policy Instrument" (RiR 2025:19).

The National Audit Office reviewed whether the increases in general state grants during the COVID-19 pandemic were designed effectively as a stabilizing policy instrument. According to the National Audit Office, the increases could have been designed in a more effective way, for example, the communication and the allocation of the grants could have been more targeted. The National Audit Office also believes that the government's explanation lacks transparency. The National Audit Office provides several recommendations to the government for future increases in general state grants for stabilization purposes.

The government constantly strives to design appropriate measures. The National Audit Office's observations and recommendations will be useful in this work. In the event of a crisis, the government will consider various forms of stabilizing policy instruments to support municipalities and regions, if necessary. The government intends to develop the overall principles of stabilizing policy in its upcoming government communication on the fiscal framework.

With this document, the government considers the National Audit Office's report to be finalized.
tldr: The Swedish National Audit Office has reviewed how the state provided additional funds to municipalities and regions during the pandemic. They believe that the government could have done so in a more effective and transparent manner.
lattlast:
The Swedish National Audit Office is an agency that monitors how the state uses its funds. They have investigated the additional funds, known as general grants, that the government provided to municipalities and regions during the COVID-19 pandemic. The purpose of these funds was to help maintain economic stability during the crisis.

According to the report, the government could have done a better job of explaining how the funds should be used. The National Audit Office also found it difficult to see exactly how the funds were distributed because the information was not clear enough. They therefore offer suggestions on how the government should proceed during the next crisis.

The government responds that they always try to make good decisions and that they will use the recommendations from the National Audit Office in their future work. During major crises, the government can choose different ways to provide financial support to municipalities and regions to help them cope with difficult times.

The government is now planning to develop its rules for how financial support should be provided in the future. This document concludes the handling of the National Audit Office's report.
